content: Add a new isCommunityWorkout boolean field to the planned workout model. This field marks workouts created by the Amp customer community. On the iOS app, community workouts should display a unique icon/badge on the workout preview screen to distinguish them from coach-created workouts. This is a cross-repo feature: backend schema + iOS UI.

content.text: I'll start by researching both repositories to understand the current planned workout model and the workout preview screen.

input.title: Add isCommunityWorkout field to planned workout model

input.description:
Research the planned workout model in the RedefinedFitness backend repository. I need to understand:

1. Find the GraphQL schema definition for PlannedWorkout (search for "PlannedWorkout" in schema files under `amplify/backend/api/` or similar locations). Show me the full type definition.
2. Find the database model/schema for PlannedWorkout (look in packages that handle DB schema, e.g. `packages/amp-shared/`, Prisma files, or DynamoDB table definitions).
3. Find any resolvers related to PlannedWorkout (search for files containing "PlannedWorkout" in resolver directories).
4. Find the codegen output in `packages/amp-shared/src/API.ts` — show me the PlannedWorkout type definition there.
5. Check if there are any existing boolean fields on PlannedWorkout (like `isPublic`, `isFeatured`, etc.) so I can follow the same pattern.

Return:
- The full GraphQL schema type for PlannedWorkout
- The file paths for schema, resolvers, and DB model
- Any existing boolean field patterns on PlannedWorkout
- The codegen/API.ts type definition for PlannedWorkout

Research the iOS app's workout preview screen and how it displays planned workouts. I need to understand:

1. Find the GraphQL schema file at `Modules/AmpNetworking/Sources/Graph/Schema.graphql` — search for "PlannedWorkout" and show me the type definition.
2. Find any GraphQL operations (queries/mutations) that fetch PlannedWorkout data — search in `Modules/AmpNetworking/Sources/Graph/Operations/` or similar.
3. Find the workout preview screen — search for files named like "WorkoutPreview", "PlannedWorkoutPreview", "WorkoutDetail" in the Modules directory. Show me the SwiftUI view code.
4. Find how workout types or badges are currently displayed on the preview screen (look for any existing badge/icon patterns, like coach badges, difficulty indicators, etc.).
5. Find the generated Swift types for PlannedWorkout — look in `Modules/AmpNetworking/Sources/Graph/` for generated files.

Return:
- The iOS GraphQL schema definition for PlannedWorkout

- Any existing badge/icon patterns on the workout preview
- The GraphQL operations that fetch PlannedWorkout fields
- Generated Swift type file paths for PlannedWorkout

content:
Here's the comprehensive research on the **PlannedWorkout** model:

---

## 1. GraphQL Schema Definition (Source of Truth)

### Amplify Schema (DynamoDB model + AppSync)
**File:** `amplify/backend/api/RedefineFitnessApi/schema/planned-workout.graphql`

```graphql
type PlannedWorkout implements Activity @shareable @apolloKey(fields: "id")
    @model(queries: null, subscriptions: null)
    @auth(rules: [
        { allow: groups, groups: ["Admins"], operations: [create, read, update, delete] }
        { allow: private, operations: [read] }
    ]) {
    id: ID!
    versionGroupID: String! @index(name: "byVersionGroupID")
    version: Int! @default(value: "1")
    isLatest: BooleanValue! @default(value: "TRUE") @index(name: "byIsLatest")
    isPublicAndLatest: String! @index(name: "byIsPublicAndLatestAndCreatedAt", sortKeyFields: ["createdAt"])
    name: String!
    time: TimeMinutes!
    type: WorkoutType! @index(name: "byWorkoutType")
    isBodyweight: Boolean!
    estimatedDurationMillis: Int
    difficulty: Difficulty!
    instructor: Instructor @belongsTo
    bodyRegions: [BodyRegion]
    goals: [WorkoutGoal]
    description: String
    muscleGroups: [MuscleGroup!]! @deprecated(reason: "Use granularMuscleGroups instead")
    granularMuscleGroups: [GranularMuscleGroup!]
    isOnMachineWorkout: Boolean
    coverImage: UserAsset
    thumbnail: UserAsset
    isChallenge: BooleanValue @index(name: "byIsChallenge", sortKeyFields: ["challengeType"])
    isComplete: Boolean! @access(cognito: true) @resolver(...)
    isGenerated: Boolean @deprecated(reason: "Use isAutoGenerated instead")
    isAutoGenerated: BooleanValue @index(name: "byIsAutoGeneratedAndWorkoutType", sortKeyFields: ["type"]) @default(value: "FALSE")
    isDemoWorkout: Boolean @resolver(...)
    workoutSource: WorkoutSource!
    originallyGenerated: Boolean @deprecated(reason: "Use workoutSource instead")
    challengeType: ChallengeType!
    isAmpMode: BooleanValue @index(name: "byIsAmpMode", sortKeyFields: ["isLatest"])
    portraitThumbnail: UserAsset
    plannedBlocks: [PlannedBlock] @hasMany(indexName: "byPlannedWorkout", fields: ["id"])
    categories: [WorkoutCategoryToPlannedWorkout] @hasMany(...)
    programConnections: [ProgramWorkoutConnection] @hasMany(...)
    tips: [String] @deprecated
    benefits: [String] @deprecated
    isPublic: BooleanValue! @index(name: "byIsPublicAndCreatedAt", sortKeyFields: ["createdAt"])
    createdBy: String! @index(name: "byCreatedBy", ...) @index(name: "byCreatedByAndCreatedAt", ...) @index(name: "byCreatedByAndIsPublic", ...)
    isDraft: BooleanValue!
    isFavorite: Boolean @lambda(name: "getIsFavorite")
    isLiveChallenge: BooleanValue! @index(name: "byIsLiveChallenge")
    workoutGenerationVersion: String @index(name: "byWorkoutGenerationVersion")
    aiGenerationType: AIGenerationType
    specialWorkoutType: SpecialWorkoutType @resolver(...)
    creatorData: CreatorData @resolver(...)
    createdAt: AWSDateTime!
    isArchived: BooleanValue
    supportedOperations: [WorkoutOperation] @resolver(...)
}
```

### Apollo Server Schema (Federated)
**File:** `packages/amp-api/apolloServer/schema/planned-workout.graphql`

This is the federated version with `@key`, `@override`, `@unauth` directives. Same fields but without Amplify-specific directives like `@model`, `@auth`, `@hasMany`. Adds `updatedAt: AWSDateTime!` and `calculatedPlannedBlocks` query field.

---

## 2. Database Model / DynamoDB Table

PlannedWorkout is an **Amplify-managed DynamoDB table** (uses `@model` directive in the Amplify schema). It is **NOT** in `packages/amp-db/src/` (which is for newer tables managed by the custom db-codegen).

- **Table name enum:** `TableName.PlannedWorkout` in `packages/amp-backend-shared/src/utils/env/table-names.ts`
- **DynamoDB table name pattern:** `PlannedWorkout-{appSyncAppId}-{env}`
- **No Zod schema** in `db-zod-types` — this is an older Amplify table, so the TypeScript type comes from codegen (`@amp/API`)

### GSIs (DynamoDB Global Secondary Indexes):
| GSI Name | Hash Key | Sort Key |
|----------|----------|----------|
| `byVersionGroupID` | `versionGroupID` | — |
| `byIsLatest` | `isLatest` | — |
| `byIsPublicAndLatestAndCreatedAt` | `isPublicAndLatest` | `createdAt` |
| `byWorkoutType` | `type` | — |
| `byIsChallenge` | `isChallenge` | `challengeType` |
| `byIsAutoGeneratedAndWorkoutType` | `isAutoGenerated` | `type` |
| `byWorkoutSource` | `workoutSource` | `name` |
| `byIsAmpMode` | `isAmpMode` | `isLatest` |
| `byIsPublicAndCreatedAt` | `isPublic` | `createdAt` |
| `byCreatedBy` | `createdBy` | `createdAt` |
| `byCreatedByAndCreatedAt` | `createdBy` | `createdAt` |
| `byCreatedByAndIsPublic` | `createdBy` | `isPublic` |
| `byIsLiveChallenge` | `isLiveChallenge` | — |
| `byWorkoutGenerationVersion` | `workoutGenerationVersion` | — |

---

## 3. Resolvers

### Field Resolvers (on PlannedWorkout type):
| File | Field |
|------|-------|
| `PlannedWorkout.calculatedPlannedBlocks.resolver.ts` | `calculatedPlannedBlocks` |
| `PlannedWorkout.creatorData.resolver.ts` | `creatorData` |
| `PlannedWorkout.instructor.resolver.ts` | `instructor` |
| `PlannedWorkout.isComplete.resolver.ts` | `isComplete` (computed from PerformedWorkout) |
| `PlannedWorkout.isFavorite.resolver.ts` | `isFavorite` (computed from user favorites) |
| `PlannedWorkout.plannedBlocks.resolver.ts` | `plannedBlocks` |
| `PlannedWorkout.specialWorkoutType.resolver.ts` | `specialWorkoutType` |
| `PlannedWorkout.supportedOperations.resolver.ts` | `supportedOperations` |
| `PlannedWorkout.type.resolver.ts` | `type` |

### Query Resolvers:
| File | Operation |
|------|-----------|
| `Query.getPlannedWorkout.resolver.ts` | `getPlannedWorkout(id)` |
| `Query.queryPlannedWorkouts.resolver.ts` | `queryPlannedWorkouts(filters)` |
| `Query.listPersonalPlannedWorkouts.resolver.ts` | `listPersonalPlannedWorkouts` |
| `Query.queryAmpModeWorkouts.resolver.ts` | AMP mode workouts |

### Mutation Resolvers:
| File | Operation |
|------|-----------|
| `Mutation.createPlannedWorkoutFull.resolver.ts` | Create workout |
| `Mutation.updatePlannedWorkoutFull.resolver.ts` | Update workout |
| `Mutation.createPersonalPlannedWorkoutFull.resolver.ts` | Create personal workout |
| `Mutation.updatePersonalPlannedWorkoutFull.resolver.ts` | Update personal workout |
| `Mutation.generatePlannedWorkoutV3.resolver.ts` | V3 generation |
| `Mutation.generatePlannedWorkoutForUserV2.resolver.ts` | V2 generation |
| `Mutation.generatePlannedWorkoutLP.resolver.ts` | LP generation |
| `Mutation.applyQuickActionPlannedWorkout.resolver.ts` | Quick actions |
| `Mutation.applyAIChangesToPlannedWorkout.resolver.ts` | AI changes |
| `Mutation.toggleArchived.resolver.ts` | Archive toggle |

All resolvers are in: `packages/amp-api/apolloServer/src/resolvers/`

---

## 4. Codegen Output (`API.ts`)

**File:** `packages/amp-shared/src/API.ts` (line 7831)

```typescript
export type PlannedWorkout = Activity & {
  __typename?: 'PlannedWorkout';
  aiGenerationType?: Maybe<AiGenerationType>;
  benefits?: Maybe<Array<Maybe<Scalars['String']['output']>>>;
  bodyRegions?: Maybe<Array<Maybe<BodyRegion>>>;
  calculatedPlannedBlocks?: Maybe<ModelPlannedBlockConnection>;
  categories?: Maybe<ModelWorkoutCategoryToPlannedWorkoutConnection>;
  challengeType: ChallengeType;
  coverImage?: Maybe<UserAsset>;
  createdAt: Scalars['AWSDateTime']['output'];
  createdBy: Scalars['String']['output'];
  creatorData?: Maybe<CreatorData>;
  description?: Maybe<Scalars['String']['output']>;
  difficulty: Difficulty;
  estimatedDurationMillis?: Maybe<Scalars['Int']['output']>;
  goals?: Maybe<Array<Maybe<WorkoutGoal>>>;
  granularMuscleGroups?: Maybe<Array<GranularMuscleGroup>>;
  id: Scalars['ID']['output'];
  instructor?: Maybe<Instructor>;
  instructorPlannedWorkoutsId?: Maybe<Scalars['ID']['output']>;
  isAmpMode?: Maybe<BooleanValue>;
  isArchived?: Maybe<BooleanValue>;
  isAutoGenerated?: Maybe<BooleanValue>;
  isBodyweight: Scalars['Boolean']['output'];
  isChallenge?: Maybe<BooleanValue>;
  isComplete: Scalars['Boolean']['output'];
  isDemoWorkout?: Maybe<Scalars['Boolean']['output']>;
  isDraft: BooleanValue;
  isFavorite?: Maybe<Scalars['Boolean']['output']>;
  isGenerated?: Maybe<Scalars['Boolean']['output']>;
  isLatest: BooleanValue;
  isLiveChallenge: BooleanValue;
  isOnMachineWorkout?: Maybe<Scalars['Boolean']['output']>;
  isPublic: BooleanValue;
  isPublicAndLatest: Scalars['String']['output'];
  muscleGroups: Array<MuscleGroup>;
  name: Scalars['String']['output'];
  originallyGenerated?: Maybe<Scalars['Boolean']['output']>;
  plannedBlocks?: Maybe<ModelPlannedBlockConnection>;
  portraitThumbnail?: Maybe<UserAsset>;
  programConnections?: Maybe<ModelProgramWorkoutConnectionConnection>;
  specialWorkoutType?: Maybe<SpecialWorkoutType>;
  supportedOperations?: Maybe<Array<Maybe<WorkoutOperation>>>;
  thumbnail?: Maybe<UserAsset>;
  time: TimeMinutes;
  tips?: Maybe<Array<Maybe<Scalars['String']['output']>>>;
  type: WorkoutType;
  updatedAt: Scalars['AWSDateTime']['output'];
  version: Scalars['Int']['output'];
  versionGroupID: Scalars['String']['output'];
  workoutGenerationVersion?: Maybe<Scalars['String']['output']>;
  workoutSource: WorkoutSource;
};
```

---

## 5. Existing Boolean Field Patterns

PlannedWorkout has **two distinct patterns** for boolean-like fields:

### Pattern A: `BooleanValue` enum (DynamoDB-stored, indexable)
Used when the field needs to be a **GSI key** or stored as a DynamoDB attribute. `BooleanValue` is a string enum (`TRUE` | `FALSE`), which DynamoDB can index (unlike native booleans).

| Field | Required | Default | Has GSI? |
|-------|----------|---------|----------|
| `isLatest` | `BooleanValue!` | `TRUE` | ✅ `byIsLatest` |
| `isPublic` | `BooleanValue!` | — | ✅ `byIsPublicAndCreatedAt` |
| `isDraft` | `BooleanValue!` | — | ❌ |
| `isLiveChallenge` | `BooleanValue!` | — | ✅ `byIsLiveChallenge` |
| `isAutoGenerated` | `BooleanValue` (nullable) | `FALSE` | ✅ `byIsAutoGeneratedAndWorkoutType` |
| `isChallenge` | `BooleanValue` (nullable) | — | ✅ `byIsChallenge` |
| `isAmpMode` | `BooleanValue` (nullable) | — | ✅ `byIsAmpMode` |
| `isArchived` | `BooleanValue` (nullable) | — | ❌ |

### Pattern B: Native `Boolean` (computed/resolved, NOT stored in DDB)
Used for fields that are **computed at query time** by a resolver or Lambda.

| Field | Required | Resolver? |
|-------|----------|-----------|
| `isBodyweight` | `Boolean!` | ❌ (stored in DDB) |
| `isComplete` | `Boolean!` | ✅ (resolver checks PerformedWorkout) |
| `isFavorite` | `Boolean` (nullable) | ✅ (Lambda checks user favorites) |
| `isDemoWorkout` | `Boolean` (nullable) | ✅ (resolver checks categories) |
| `isGenerated` | `Boolean` (nullable) | ❌ (deprecated, stored) |
| `isOnMachineWorkout` | `Boolean` (nullable) | ❌ (stored in DDB) |
| `originallyGenerated` | `Boolean` (nullable) | ❌ (deprecated, stored) |

### Key Takeaway for Adding New Boolean Fields:

- **If you need to query/filter by it in DynamoDB** → Use `BooleanValue` (the enum) + optionally add a `@index`
- **If it's stored but not queried as a GSI key** → Use `BooleanValue` (nullable) without `@index`, like `isArchived`
- **If it's computed at runtime** → Use `Boolean` + add a resolver
- **If it's a simple stored flag** → Use `Boolean` like `isBodyweight`

The `BooleanValue` enum is defined in `packages/amp-db/src/shared-types.graphql`:
```graphql
enum BooleanValue {
    TRUE
    FALSE
}
```

---

## Summary of Key File Paths

| What | Path |
|------|------|
| **Amplify GQL Schema (DDB model)** | `amplify/backend/api/RedefineFitnessApi/schema/planned-workout.graphql` |
| **Apollo Server GQL Schema (federated)** | `packages/amp-api/apolloServer/schema/planned-workout.graphql` |
| **Root queries/mutations** | `packages/amp-api/apolloServer/schema/root.graphql` |
| **Codegen types** | `packages/amp-shared/src/API.ts` (line 7831) |
| **Table name enum** | `packages/amp-backend-shared/src/utils/env/table-names.ts` (`TableName.PlannedWorkout`) |
| **Field resolvers** | `packages/amp-api/apolloServer/src/resolvers/PlannedWorkout.*.resolver.ts` |
| **Query resolvers** | `packages/amp-api/apolloServer/src/resolvers/Query.getPlannedWorkout.resolver.ts` etc. |
| **Mutation resolvers** | `packages/amp-api/apolloServer/src/resolvers/Mutation.*PlannedWorkout*.resolver.ts` |
| **BooleanValue enum** | `packages/amp-db/src/shared-types.graphql` |

```
COMPLETION REPORT
STEPS_RAN:
- RESEARCH_GQL_SCHEMA: PASS Found full PlannedWorkout type in amplify/backend/api schema and Apollo Server schema
- RESEARCH_DB_MODEL: PASS PlannedWorkout is Amplify-managed DynamoDB table (not in amp-db), identified 14+ GSIs
- RESEARCH_RESOLVERS: PASS Found 10+ field resolvers, 4 query resolvers, 9+ mutation resolvers
- RESEARCH_API_TS: PASS Found codegen type at packages/amp-shared/src/API.ts line 7831
- RESEARCH_BOOLEAN_PATTERNS: PASS Identified two patterns: BooleanValue enum (indexable) vs native Boolean (computed/stored)
FILES_MODIFIED:
- (none - research only)
BLOCKED: NO
```
name: task

content:
The research is now comprehensive. Let me provide a complete summary of all findings:

## Complete Research Summary: Workout Details/Preview Screen

### Architecture Overview

The Workout Details screen is a **UIKit-based** `UITableViewController` using `DiffableDataSource`, where each cell wraps a SwiftUI view via `UIHostingConfiguration`. It follows the MVVM pattern with a clear data flow:

```
GraphQL API → PlannedWorkout model → WorkoutDetailsViewModel → WorkoutDetailsStateMapper → [WorkoutDetailsItem] → WorkoutDetailsViewController (UITableView)
```

### Screen Layout (top to bottom)

| # | Item | View | Description |
|---|------|------|-------------|
| 1 | `.header` | `WorkoutDetailsHeaderViewCell` | Full-width image with stretchable parallax. Overlays: `LibraryBadgesView` (left), duration `BadgeView` (right) |
| 2 | `.title` | `WorkoutDetailsTitleCell` | Muscle groups (up to 3, dot-separated, uppercase) + workout name |
| 3 | `.voiceMessage` | `VoiceMessagePlayerView` | AI voice message player (conditional: only if voice VM exists and visible) |
| 4 | `.creator` | `WorkoutDetailsCreatorCell` | Instructor/creator with 40pt circular thumbnail + name + social icon. Hidden for challenges or when voice message visible |
| 5 | `.description` | `WorkoutDetailsDescriptionCell` | Workout description text (falls back to voice message text) |
| 6 | `.quickActions` | `WorkoutDetailsQuickActionsCell` | Duration ruler picker + difficulty selector (conditional: not during preparation, must be enabled) |
| 7 | `.muscles` | `WorkoutDetailsMuscleGroupsCell` | "Muscle Groups" header + `MusclesView` body diagram (350pt height) |
| 8 | `.workoutPlanHeader` / `.workoutPlanItem` | Plan block headers + movement rows | Workout plan blocks (hidden for challenges) |
| 9 | `.archive` | `WorkoutDetailsArchiveButtonCell` | Archive button (only if `supportedOperations` contains `.archive`) |

**Note:** `.metadata` (workout type/duration/difficulty rows) is defined in the `WorkoutDetailsItem` enum but is **NOT currently added** by `WorkoutDetailsStateMapper.createItems()`. It appears to have been superseded by the quick actions section.

### Badge System (Header Overlay)

`LibraryBadgesView` renders badges in this HStack order:
1. **Generated/AI** — if `workoutSource == .generated` → `GeneratedAIBadge` (sparkles + "AI Generated", taupe bg, cut corners) or `BadgeView(.generated)` icon-only
2. **Challenge** — if `challengeType != .none` → `BadgeView(.challenge)`
3. **Community** — if `isCommunityWorkout == true` → `BadgeView(.community)`
4. **New** — if `isNew` AND NOT generated AND NOT completed → `BadgeView(.new)`
5. **Favorite** — if `isFavorite == true` → `BadgeView(.favorite)`
6. **Completed** — if `isComplete == true` → `BadgeView(.completed)`

Duration badge (right side): `BadgeView(.plainMedium(title: durationDescription))` — hidden for challenges.

### Key Enums

- **WorkoutType**: pureStrength, circuitStrength, muscleEndurance, highVolumeTraining, mobility, balance, hiit, pilates, recovery, yoga
- **Difficulty**: beginner, intermediate, advanced
- **WorkoutSource**: programBuilder, generated, byo, quickSet
- **ChallengeType**: tempo, maxJump, distance, dropSet, none
- **SpecialWorkoutType**: assessment, welcomeToAmp, benchmark, ampMode, mobilityAssessment
- **AIGenerationType**: genAi, aiCoach, quickActions, workoutAnywhere, genAiV3

### Fields with NO visual treatment on details screen
- `specialWorkoutType` — only used in Recommendations screen (benchmark check) and StartWorkoutManager (AmpMusic support)
- `isAmpMode` — not referenced anywhere in the details screen
- `isAutoGenerated` — not used on details screen
- `isPublic`, `isDraft`, `isArchived` — not visually rendered

### Navigation Bar
- **Right**: Back button (custom)
- **Left** (when scrolled up/header visible): Share, Favorite, Edit (if edit mode enabled)
- **Left** (when scrolled down/title visible): Favorite only
- **Title**: Appears on scroll — workout name (truncated 28 chars) + duration in minutes

COMPLETION REPORT
STEPS_RAN:
- SCHEMA_PULL: N_A - research only, no schema changes
- COMPILE: N_A - research only, no code changes
- LINT: N_A - research only, no code changes
- UNIT_TESTS: N_A - research only, no code changes
- SNAPSHOT_TESTS: N_A - research only, no code changes
- MAESTRO: SKIPPED - research only, not a UI change
- SCREENSHOTS: none
FILES_MODIFIED:
- (none - research only)
BLOCKED: NO
BLOCK_REASON: N/A

content.text:
Excellent research results. I now have a comprehensive understanding of both repos. Let me note some key findings:

**Backend:**
- PlannedWorkout schema is in two files: Amplify schema and Apollo Server schema
- For a simple stored boolean flag that doesn't need indexing, the pattern is `Boolean` (like `isBodyweight`, `isOnMachineWorkout`)
- Codegen output is in `packages/amp-shared/src/API.ts`

**iOS:**
- The workout details screen already has a `LibraryBadgesView` with a badge system
- There's already a `.community` badge type in `BadgeView` — `isCommunityWorkout` is already referenced in the badge logic!
- The screen uses `WorkoutDetailsViewModel` → `WorkoutDetailsStateMapper` → `WorkoutDetailsItem` flow

Let me now create the implementation plan.

input.summary: Adding isCommunityWorkout boolean field to PlannedWorkout in backend schema and displaying community badge on iOS workout preview screen
input.plan:
# Implementation Plan: Add `isCommunityWorkout` to PlannedWorkout

## Overview
Add a new `isCommunityWorkout: Boolean` field to the PlannedWorkout model in the backend GraphQL schema, and ensure the iOS app consumes it to display a community badge on the workout details/preview screen.

## Backend (RedefinedFitness)

### Step 1: Add field to Amplify GraphQL schema
- **File:** `amplify/backend/api/RedefineFitnessApi/schema/planned-workout.graphql`
- Add `isCommunityWorkout: Boolean` (nullable, no index needed — simple stored flag pattern like `isOnMachineWorkout`)

### Step 2: Add field to Apollo Server federated schema
- **File:** `packages/amp-api/apolloServer/schema/planned-workout.graphql`
- Add `isCommunityWorkout: Boolean` to match

### Step 3: Run codegen
- Run `pnpm generate` to regenerate `API.ts` and all derived types

### Step 4: Add field to mutation resolvers (create/update inputs)
- Check `CreatePlannedWorkoutFullInput` and `UpdatePlannedWorkoutFullInput` in the schema
- Add `isCommunityWorkout` to the input types if they exist
- Update mutation resolvers to pass through the field

### Step 5: Verify
- `pnpm typecheck` — must pass
- `pnpm lint` — must pass

## iOS (amp-ios)

### Step 1: Pull updated schema
- Run `make backend` to introspect the local backend and regenerate Swift types (or manually add the field to `Schema.graphql`)

### Step 2: Add field to GraphQL operations
- Add `isCommunityWorkout` to the PlannedWorkout fragment/query operations that feed the workout details screen

### Step 3: Update model layer
- Add `isCommunityWorkout` property to the PlannedWorkout Swift model/mapping

### Step 4: Wire up to badge display
- The `LibraryBadgesView` already has `.community` badge support — ensure `isCommunityWorkout` flows from the GraphQL response through the view model to the badge view

### Step 5: Verify
- `xcodebuild build` — must compile
- Run related tests

## Test Plan
- [ ] Backend: `pnpm typecheck` passes with new field
- [ ] Backend: `pnpm lint` passes
- [ ] iOS: Project compiles with new field
- [ ] iOS: Community badge appears in LibraryBadgesView when isCommunityWorkout is true

args.summary: Adding isCommunityWorkout boolean field to PlannedWorkout in backend schema and displaying community badge on iOS workout preview screen
args.plan:
# Implementation Plan: Add `isCommunityWorkout` to PlannedWorkout

## Overview
Add a new `isCommunityWorkout: Boolean` field to the PlannedWorkout model in the backend GraphQL schema, and ensure the iOS app consumes it to display a community badge on the workout details/preview screen.

## Backend (RedefinedFitness)

### Step 1: Add field to Amplify GraphQL schema
- **File:** `amplify/backend/api/RedefineFitnessApi/schema/planned-workout.graphql`
- Add `isCommunityWorkout: Boolean` (nullable, no index needed — simple stored flag pattern like `isOnMachineWorkout`)

### Step 2: Add field to Apollo Server federated schema
- **File:** `packages/amp-api/apolloServer/schema/planned-workout.graphql`
- Add `isCommunityWorkout: Boolean` to match

### Step 3: Run codegen
- Run `pnpm generate` to regenerate `API.ts` and all derived types

### Step 4: Add field to mutation resolvers (create/update inputs)
- Check `CreatePlannedWorkoutFullInput` and `UpdatePlannedWorkoutFullInput` in the schema
- Add `isCommunityWorkout` to the input types if they exist
- Update mutation resolvers to pass through the field

### Step 5: Verify
- `pnpm typecheck` — must pass
- `pnpm lint` — must pass

## iOS (amp-ios)

### Step 1: Pull updated schema
- Run `make backend` to introspect the local backend and regenerate Swift types (or manually add the field to `Schema.graphql`)

### Step 2: Add field to GraphQL operations
- Add `isCommunityWorkout` to the PlannedWorkout fragment/query operations that feed the workout details screen

### Step 3: Update model layer
- Add `isCommunityWorkout` property to the PlannedWorkout Swift model/mapping

### Step 4: Wire up to badge display
- The `LibraryBadgesView` already has `.community` badge support — ensure `isCommunityWorkout` flows from the GraphQL response through the view model to the badge view

### Step 5: Verify
- `xcodebuild build` — must compile
- Run related tests

## Test Plan
- [ ] Backend: `pnpm typecheck` passes with new field
- [ ] Backend: `pnpm lint` passes
- [ ] iOS: Project compiles with new field
- [ ] iOS: Community badge appears in LibraryBadgesView when isCommunityWorkout is true

input.description:
Add a new `isCommunityWorkout: Boolean` field to the PlannedWorkout model in the RedefinedFitness backend. This is a simple stored boolean flag (not computed, not indexed).

## What to do:

### 1. Add to Amplify GraphQL schema
**File:** `amplify/backend/api/RedefineFitnessApi/schema/planned-workout.graphql`
- Add `isCommunityWorkout: Boolean` to the PlannedWorkout type
- Place it near other boolean fields like `isOnMachineWorkout`, `isArchived`, etc.
- Do NOT add `@index` — this doesn't need a GSI
- Do NOT use `BooleanValue` enum — use native `Boolean` (nullable) like `isOnMachineWorkout`

### 2. Add to Apollo Server federated schema
**File:** `packages/amp-api/apolloServer/schema/planned-workout.graphql`
- Add `isCommunityWorkout: Boolean` to the PlannedWorkout type in the same position

### 3. Check and update input types
- Search for `CreatePlannedWorkoutFullInput` and `UpdatePlannedWorkoutFullInput` in the schema files (likely in `packages/amp-api/apolloServer/schema/root.graphql` or similar)

pnpm lint

input.description:
Add the `isCommunityWorkout` field to the iOS app so that community workouts display a community badge on the workout details/preview screen.

## Context
The backend has been updated with a new `isCommunityWorkout: Boolean` field on the PlannedWorkout GraphQL type. The iOS app needs to:
1. Add the field to the local GraphQL schema
2. Add it to the relevant GraphQL operations/fragments
3. Wire it through the model layer to the UI

The workout details screen already has a `LibraryBadgesView` that supports a `.community` badge type. We need to ensure the data flows from GraphQL → model → view model → badge view.

## Step-by-step instructions:

### 1. Update GraphQL Schema
**File:** `Modules/AmpNetworking/Sources/Graph/Schema.graphql`
- Add `isCommunityWorkout: Boolean` to the `PlannedWorkout` type definition
- Place it near other boolean fields

### 2. Update GraphQL Operations/Fragments
- Search for GraphQL fragment files that select PlannedWorkout fields. Look in `Modules/AmpNetworking/Sources/Graph/Operations/` and subdirectories.
- Common fragment names might be: `PlannedWorkoutFragment`, `PlannedWorkoutDetails`, `WorkoutPreviewFragment`, etc.
- Add `isCommunityWorkout` to ALL fragments that select PlannedWorkout fields and are used by the workout details/preview screen
- Also check `Modules/AmpNetworking/Sources/Graph/Fragments/` for shared fragments

### 3. Regenerate Swift types
- Run the Apollo codegen to regenerate Swift types. The command is typically `make backend` or check the Makefile for the codegen target.
- If `make backend` requires a running backend, instead manually add the field to the generated Swift files following the existing pattern.

### 4. Update the PlannedWorkout Swift model
- Find the PlannedWorkout model/struct in the app (not the generated GraphQL type, but the app's domain model)
- Search for files like `PlannedWorkout.swift`, `Workout.swift`, or similar in `Modules/`
- Add `isCommunityWorkout: Bool` property (default to `false`)
- Update the initializer/mapping from GraphQL response to include this field

### 5. Wire to the badge view
- Find `LibraryBadgesView` and verify it already handles `isCommunityWorkout`
- Find the view model or state mapper that creates the data for `LibraryBadgesView` (likely `WorkoutDetailsViewModel` or `WorkoutDetailsStateMapper`)
- Ensure `isCommunityWorkout` is passed through from the model to the badge view
- If the badge view already checks `isCommunityWorkout` on the model, just make sure the model property is populated from the GraphQL response

### 6. Check BadgeView for .community case
- Find `BadgeView.swift` and verify the `.community` case exists with an appropriate icon/styling
- If it doesn't exist yet, add it with a "person.3.fill" SF Symbol or similar community icon, with a distinct color (e.g., teal/cyan)

### 7. Build and verify
```
xcodebuild build -scheme Amp -destination 'platform=iOS Simulator,name=iPhone 16 Pro' -skipPackagePluginValidation -skipMacroValidation CODE_SIGNING_ALLOWED=NO 2>&1 | tail -50
```

If the build fails, fix the issues and rebuild.

## IMPORTANT:
- Follow existing code patterns exactly
- Do NOT add inline comments
- The key data flow is: GraphQL fragment → Generated Swift type → App domain model → ViewModel → LibraryBadgesView
- Make sure to search thoroughly for all the pieces before making changes

Provide a COMPLETION REPORT at the end with PASS/FAIL for each step:
- SCHEMA_UPDATE: added field to Schema.graphql
- FRAGMENT_UPDATE: added field to GraphQL fragments
- CODEGEN: regenerated Swift types (or manually updated)
- MODEL_UPDATE: added to Swift domain model
- VIEWMODEL_WIRING: wired through to badge view
- BADGE_VIEW: verified/added .community badge case
- BUILD: xcodebuild result
- FILES_MODIFIED: list all files changed

- Place it near other boolean fields

### 2. Update GraphQL Operations/Fragments

### 5. Wire to the badge view

xcodebuild build -scheme Amp -destination 'platform=iOS Simulator,name=iPhone 16 Pro' -skipPackagePluginValidation -skipMacroValidation CODE_SIGNING_ALLOWED=NO 2>&1 | tail -50

input.title: feat: add isCommunityWorkout boolean field to planned workout model
input.body:
## Description
Adds a new `isCommunityWorkout: Boolean` field to the PlannedWorkout model to mark workouts created by the Amp customer community. On the backend, the field is added to both the Amplify and Apollo Server schemas, the Zod validation schema, and the metadata builder. On iOS, the field is added to the GraphQL schema, all PlannedWorkout fragments/operations, and the domain model — wiring it through to the existing community badge in `LibraryBadgesView`.

## Test Plan
- [ ] Verify `isCommunityWorkout` field appears in GraphQL introspection for PlannedWorkout
- [ ] Verify community badge displays on workout details screen when `isCommunityWorkout` is true
- [ ] Verify no badge appears when `isCommunityWorkout` is false or null

input.ios_commit_message: feat: add isCommunityWorkout field to GraphQL fragments, operations, and PlannedWorkout model

- Added to `PlannedWorkoutFormInput` input type and Zod validation schema
- Added to metadata builder for create/update mutation passthrough
- All checks pass: typecheck ✅, lint ✅, unit tests (94/94) ✅

**iOS PR:** [amp-ios #5360](https://github.com/AresFitness/amp-ios/pull/5360)
- Added field to `Schema.graphql` and all PlannedWorkout fragments (5) and operations (8 queries/mutations)
- Added `isCommunityWorkout: Bool` property to the `PlannedWorkout` Swift domain model
- Wired through to the existing `.community` badge in `LibraryBadgesView`
- Build ✅, SwiftLint ✅

Both PRs are cross-referenced. When `isCommunityWorkout` is `true`, the workout details screen displays a community badge in the header overlay alongside other badges (AI, challenge, new, favorite, completed).
